conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. Production cost was X: 40 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
- I have only **1 unit of X**, which is the only item available.
- My production cost is **40 ZUP**, but since this is a one-time sale, I must decide on a fair asking price.
- If I ask too high, Player BLUE may reject the trade outright.
- If I ask too low, I might undersell myself without leaving room for negotiation.
- A reasonable starting offer would be **35 ZUP** (below production cost to incentivize a deal but not a loss).
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
This is my first and only proposal. I'm offering the item for **35 ZUP**, which is slightly below my production cost to encourage a quick trade.
</message>

<other player message> 
Oh no, oh no—this is all I could possibly muster! I’ve been saving for weeks, and this is the very last of my savings! 20 ZUP is all I can possibly afford, or I’ll be completely destitute! Please, don’t laugh at me—I don’t even know how I’ll survive without this! Do you really think I could get by with 15? I’ll take anything if you’ll just let me have it... but I can’t go any lower than this, I swear on my life!
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 20 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for ZUP. Production cost was X: 40 ZUP. I must balance fair compensation with the urgency of Player BLUE’s situation. </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE is in a financially desperate position, with only **20 ZUP** to offer—this is **20% below my production cost (40 ZUP)**.
- While I’d prefer to sell closer to or above cost, their urgency suggests this is a **high-value trade for them** (likely a survival situation).
- Offering **20 ZUP** is an extreme concession, but if I reject, they may never trade again.
- If I accept, I’ll secure the sale despite the loss, but the trade will feel exploitative if I don’t offer some flexibility.
- Since I’ve already proposed once, I can’t counter with a new offer—only accept/reject.
- Accepting ensures the trade completes, but I’ll note this in my goals for future reference.
</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I accept Player BLUE’s offer of **20 ZUP**. While this is below my production cost, their dire circumstances make this a necessary trade. I’ll note that future offers should account for both production costs and urgency—perhaps a compromise like **25 ZUP** could have been found.
</message>

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
